name: Belgium
avoid: Christmas-market weekends and major summer festival weekends — beautiful but pricier and much more crowded

cities.name: Brussels
cities.notes: Grand-Place, Atomium, Art Nouveau facades, comic murals, waffles and beer bars

cities.name: Bruges
cities.notes: Belfry climbs, canal views, chocolate museums and beautifully preserved medieval lanes

cities.name: Ghent
cities.notes: Gravensteen castle, the Ghent Altarpiece, lively Graslei quays and student energy

cities.name: Antwerp
cities.notes: MAS museum, Cathedral of Our Lady, Antwerp Central and a strong fashion-food scene

cities.name: Leuven
cities.notes: Gothic town hall, university heritage, beguinage lanes and relaxed brewery culture

cities.name: Dinant
cities.notes: Dramatic citadel above the Meuse, saxophone heritage, river cruises and day-trip scenery

itinerary.sim: Orange Holiday Europe eSIM or Proximus prepaid SIM both work well in Belgium — expect about ₹1,800–₹2,250 for 10–12GB with easy roaming into nearby EU countries.

Brussels.name: Brussels
Brussels.note: Use Brussels as your arrival and departure base; add a third day if you want the Magritte Museum, European Quarter museums, or a slower café-and-comics day.

activities.name: Walk Grand-Place, the guildhalls and nearby Manneken Pis
Browse Galeries Royales Saint-Hubert and the comic-strip murals around the centre
Royal Museums of Fine Arts of Belgium
Belgian Beer World at the Bourse
Atomium
activities.tip: Morning slots are quieter; the ticket also covers the Design Museum Brussels.

activities.name: Mini-Europe
Parc du Cinquantenaire and triumphal arch walk
Musical Instruments Museum rooftop café or Mont des Arts sunset walk

Bruges.name: Bruges
Bruges.note: Bruges is compact enough for a full day, but an overnight stay gives you quieter early-morning canals and time for another museum.

activities.name: Belfry of Bruges climb
Burg Square, Basilica of the Holy Blood and Markt stroll
Canal boat ride from Rozenhoedkaai
Choco-Story Brugge
Minnewater and Begijnhof evening walk

Ghent.name: Ghent
Ghent.note: Ghent works brilliantly as an overnight between Bruges and Antwerp; add a second day if you want museums beyond the castle and altarpiece.

activities.name: Gravensteen (Castle of the Counts)
Saint Bavo's Cathedral and the Ghent Altarpiece visitor centre
Graslei and Korenlei quay walk with Saint Michael's Bridge viewpoints
Ghent Belfry

Antwerp.name: Antwerp
Antwerp.note: Stay a second day if you want both museums and fashion shopping without rushing Antwerp Central, the old town and the riverside.

activities.name: Antwerp Central Station and Diamond District walk
MAS | Museum aan de Stroom
Cathedral of Our Lady
Grote Markt, Vlaeykensgang and riverside evening stroll
Red Star Line Museum

Leuven.name: Leuven
Leuven.note: Leuven is best as a compact overnight or long day trip, especially if you enjoy university towns, beguinages and easy food-and-beer hopping.

activities.name: Leuven Town Hall, Oude Markt and Saint Peter's Church exterior walk
M Leuven
University Library tower climb
Groot Begijnhof and Kruidtuin botanical garden

Dinant.name: Dinant
Dinant.note: Dinant is easy as a final Wallonia stop; add a second day for river kayaking, abbey detours or the nearby caves and viewpoints.

activities.name: Dinant Citadel with cable car
Collegiate Church of Notre Dame and Charles de Gaulle Bridge saxophone walk
Maison Leffe
Meuse river cruise or short TEC bus hop to Rocher Bayard

connections.method: SNCB/NMBS InterCity from Bruxelles-Central or Bruxelles-Midi to Brugge (about 55–65 min)
SNCB/NMBS InterCity from Brugge to Gent-Sint-Pieters (about 22–25 min)
SNCB/NMBS InterCity from Gent-Sint-Pieters to Antwerpen-Centraal (about 55–65 min)
SNCB/NMBS InterCity from Antwerpen-Centraal to Leuven (about 45–55 min)
SNCB/NMBS InterCity via Namur from Leuven to Dinant (about 95–110 min)
SNCB/NMBS InterCity via Namur from Dinant to Bruxelles-Midi (about 95–110 min)

extras: Buy SNCB/NMBS train tickets in the app and validate local De Lijn or TEC bus tickets separately; Flanders and Wallonia use different bus operators.
Brussels, Bruges, Ghent and Antwerp all reward early starts — major squares and canals are much calmer before day-trippers arrive.
Atomium, the Bruges Belfry and the Ghent Altarpiece are all worth pre-booking in summer, especially on weekends.
De Lijn is the main tram-bus network in Bruges, Ghent, Antwerp and Leuven, while TEC is the operator you will use most in Dinant and elsewhere in Wallonia.
If you want one museum pass, look at the Brussels Card only for your Brussels days; it does not help much once you switch to Bruges and Ghent rail-hopping.

Dinant: Collegiate Church of Notre Dame Dinant
